activities_projects.title: Universal Indicator and pH Testing
activities_projects.objective: Determine the relative strength of various acids and bases
steps: Place 2ml of each solution in separate test tubes
Add 2 drops of universal indicator to each
Match the resulting color with the standard pH color chart
Classify each as a strong acid, weak acid, strong base, or weak base
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between strong and weak electrolytes based on hydrogen ion concentration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct correlation of color to pH value and strength classification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conductivity of Acidic Solutions
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why some liquids conduct electricity while others do not
steps: Fix two nails on a cork and place it in a beaker
Connect the nails to a battery and bulb in series
Fill the beaker with glucose and switch on; observe the bulb
Repeat with alcohol, then with dilute HCl
Note in which case the bulb glows
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that acids dissociate into ions in water to conduct electricity, unlike glucose or alcohol
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why ions are necessary for electrical conductivity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reaction of Acids with Metal Carbonates
activities_projects.objective: Study the evolution and testing of Carbon Dioxide gas
steps: Take 0.5g of sodium carbonate in a test tube and add 2ml of dilute HCl
Pass the gas evolved through the delivery tube into lime water
Observe the change in lime water (milky appearance)
Continue passing the gas for a longer time and observe the change again
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the formation of Calcium Bicarbonate which dissolves the milkiness in excess $CO_2$
activities_projects.evaluation: Writing both chemical equations for the lime water test (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Role of Water in Acidic Behavior
activities_projects.objective: Observe if acids show acidic properties in the absence of water
steps: Prepare HCl gas by reacting NaCl with conc. $H_2SO_4$ in a test tube
Bring dry blue litmus paper near the mouth of the delivery tube
Bring moist blue litmus paper near the mouth of the delivery tube
Compare the color changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that $H^+$ ions are only produced in the presence of water
activities_projects.evaluation: Reasoning for why dry HCl gas does not change the color of dry litmus (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Enthalpy of Dilution
activities_projects.objective: Understand the correct method of diluting a concentrated acid
steps: Take 10ml of water in a beaker
Slowly add a few drops of conc. $H_2SO_4$ along the sides of the beaker while stirring
Measure the temperature change
Discuss why acid is added to water and not vice versa
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the exothermic nature of dilution and the safety hazards of acid splashing
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the safety protocol for diluting concentrated acids (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Self-Made Red Cabbage Indicator
activities_projects.objective: Prepare and standardize a complex natural indicator
steps: Boil chopped red cabbage in water until the water turns dark purple
Filter the liquid to get the indicator
Test the indicator with vinegar (acid) and baking soda (base)
Record the color spectrum (Red/Pink for acid, Green/Yellow for base)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns about anthocyanins and their sensitivity to pH changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Creation of a color-reference chart for the cabbage indicator (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: pH of Soil Samples
activities_projects.objective: Test the acidity of soil to determine plant suitability
steps: Mix 2g of soil with 5ml of distilled water in a test tube
Shake well and filter the contents
Check the pH of the filtrate using universal indicator paper
Research which plants grow best in the measured pH
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the concept of pH to agriculture and environmental science
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison report of soil pH from at least two different areas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chlor-Alkali Process Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand the industrial production of $NaOH, Cl_2, 	ext{ and } H_2$
steps: Electrolyze the brine solution using the carbon electrodes
Add a drop of phenolphthalein near the cathode
Observe the pink color formation and the smell of chlorine at the anode
Draw a diagram of the process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the products formed at the anode and cathode during brine electrolysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct chemical equation for the Chlor-alkali process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water of Crystallization Quantification
activities_projects.objective: Determine the percentage of water in Washing Soda
steps: Weight 5g of washing soda in a crucible
Heat it strongly for 5-10 minutes until it becomes a fine powder (soda ash)
Allow it to cool and reweigh
Calculate the mass loss and relate it to the 10 molecules of water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between hydrated and anhydrous salts through mass analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Calculation of the percentage of water lost during heating (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Antacid Neutralization Capacity
activities_projects.objective: Compare how different antacids neutralize stomach acid
materials: Two different brands of antacid tablets
steps: Crush equal masses of two different antacid tablets
Dissolve each in a fixed amount of water
Add phenolphthalein to both (they should turn pink)
Add dilute HCl drop by drop until the pink color disappears
Count which antacid required more drops of acid to neutralize
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the effectiveness of bases like $Mg(OH)_2$ or $Al(OH)_3$ in neutralizing acids
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative analysis of antacid strength based on drops of acid used (10 marks)
